text:
Once upon a recent Tuesday, in a quaint little town hidden behind whispering woods and over the hilltops, lived an old bear named Gus. Gus had more white hair than anyone else in town and a personality as warm as honey. He made his home in an antique tree stump just outside the cozy village square.

Gus was a mystery. He didn't mingle much—or perhaps he didn't have anyone to mingle with. Every so often, you'd hear his deep, contented snores coming from his stump den, but those were mostly just the wind waking him.

One chilly afternoon, with a gentle breeze sweeping through the copse, a young fox named Leo wandered into Gus's world. Leo was curious about every creature in the forest but had never crossed paths with an old bear. Gus invited him into his stump home for tea, something Leo hadn't ever had before.

Their conversation flowed as easily as tea in a mug. Gus regaled Leo with stories of the forest, each
